danger_signals.visual_form: Mine objects placed on playing surface
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard that threatens player safety; collision triggers danger
Weaponized game object; danger increases when opposing team gains possession

danger_signals.visual_form: Dead player bodies remain visible on ice
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal hazard environment; corpses become obstacles and visual markers of danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapons and objects falling onto ice from crowd
danger_signals.meaning: Unpredictable environmental threat; ambient danger from spectators

danger_signals.visual_form: Large demon head replacing standard net; explodes on goal
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger scoring condition; visual aggression indicator

safety_signals.visual_form: Beneficial items scattered on ice by spectators
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity for player advantage; collectible safety resources
Temporary safety from hazards; consequence space but protective removal from active play

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanical/artificial appearance; distinct from organic mutants
Skeletal, bone-based visual design; recognizable as undead mutant species
Creature-based mutant design; distinct morphology from robots and skeletons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hazards (mines, holes) visible on ice; corpses accumulating; crowd throwing weapons
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players navigate around obstacles; frequent player elimination; combat engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant slug ice resurfacer appears; debris and corpses removed from playing surface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arena reset; environmental cleansing; preparation for next period

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fight minigame initiated; one-on-one combat presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Knockout objective; penalty box assignment for loser; winner gains advantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demon head explodes upon goal contact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defensive mechanism activation; high visual feedback for successful scoring

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skull rating system (0-6 skulls) displays team tier
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Higher skull teams presumed more dangerous/capable; visual hierarchy of opponent threat

environmental_storytelling.example: Dead player bodies remain on ice surface until slug resurfacer removes them, creating visual narrative of danger and showing accumulation of casualties during play
Mine and hole placement on ice visually conveys lethality of arena; denser hazard distribution signals more dangerous match conditions

environmental_storytelling.technique: Crowd participation as world state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Spectators throwing weapons and powerups communicates living, chaotic arena environment; spectator behavior reflects game intensity
Substituting standard goalie for large demon head creates visual escalation; explosion animation signals consequence and danger amplification
Giant slug performing ice maintenance between periods reinforces mutant-filled grimdark setting and explains debris cleanup through in-world mechanics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement; collision-based interaction with hazards
Increased danger if puck is weaponized (exploding variant); strategic objective
Minigame combat mode; knockout objective; loser takes double penalty
Remains as corpse obstacle on ice; creates tripping hazard for other players
Blocks net; explodes when goal is scored on it; high visual aggression
Throws weapons and powerups onto ice unpredictably; influences game state through environmental items
Moves across ice consuming corpses and debris; resets environmental state
Can be influenced to call fake penalties against opposing team; corruption mechanic communicates rule malleability

notes: Mutant League Hockey uses visual degradation and violence as core world-state communication. The game's manifestation layer relies heavily on: (1) persistent environmental hazards that remain visible to telegraph danger, (2) faction differentiation through three distinct mutant species with inherent visual distinction, (3) corpse persistence as both mechanical hazard and narrative element showing lethality, (4) crowd interaction as dynamic world-state element, (5) dramatic visual events (explosions, slug appearance, demon goalie) to mark state transitions. The skull ranking system (0-6) provides abstract threat visualization for opponent teams. The game communicates a chaotic, violent, unpredictable arena environment through visual noise (scattered weapons, hazards, corpses) and behavior volatility (crowd interference, referee bribery).
